skill.name: brand-guidelines
skill.description: Create and audit brand guidelines for visual identity, logo usage, color systems, typography, voice, messaging, and cross-channel brand applications.
skill.summary: Create practical brand guidelines and audit materials for consistent brand identity.
skill.author: AutumnsGrove

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but manual review found documentation templates, color values, typography terms, and Markdown code fences rather than executable code. The only retained risk factor is low-severity hardcoded example URLs in a sample brand configuration; no malicious intent, prompt injection, credential access, or command execution was found.

low_findings.title: Static Network Findings Are Example URLs
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL findings are font specimen links and sample brand portal links in example configuration data. They are not used by executable code and do not send data over the network.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review shows URL values inside examples/brand-config.json only. No fetch, curl, or runtime network logic was found around these locations.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Findings Are Markdown Fences
low_findings.description: The Ruby backtick execution findings are Markdown inline code, code fences, and template examples. They document brand content formats and do not execute shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Representative flagged locations are Markdown examples and inline file references. No script files, shell invocations, or user-controlled command construction were found.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Findings Are Design Vocabulary
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings map to words and values used in brand design content, including descriptions, desktop sizing, color codes, and design-system terminology. They do not reference cryptographic algorithms or security-sensitive code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Reviewed examples show ordinary brand guidance terms and numeric design specifications. No hashing, encryption, credential handling, or cryptographic APIs were present in the reviewed context.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance Findings Are Brand Process Language
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance findings are ordinary uses of words such as system, monitoring, grid system, and design system in brand governance guidance. They do not instruct the model to inspect host systems or networks.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines refer to layout grids, brand asset systems, or design systems. No host enumeration, port scanning, or network discovery behavior was found.

content.value_statement: Teams often lose consistency when brand rules live in scattered files. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code create, audit, and apply practical brand guidelines.
actual_capabilities: Creates brand guideline structures covering logo, color, typography, imagery, layout, voice, and messaging.
Provides templates for brand books, logo usage guides, style guides, and voice examples.
Audits materials against brand rules with severity ratings and remediation steps.
Documents accessibility checks for color contrast and typography readability.
Adapts brand standards across websites, social media, print, presentations, and internal communications.
Supports multi-brand and sub-brand governance workflows.
limitations: It does not generate original logo artwork by itself.
It cannot verify font licenses or asset rights without user-provided source information.
It relies on provided brand inputs for exact colors, logos, and messaging claims.
Example URLs and contacts are placeholders and need replacement before use.

use_cases.title: Create a New Brand Book
use_cases.description: Turn early brand decisions into a clear guide for logo usage, color, typography, voice, and channel rules.

use_cases.title: Audit Campaign Materials
use_cases.description: Review documents, ads, emails, and social posts for consistency issues and produce a prioritized correction plan.

use_cases.title: Standardize Design Handoffs
use_cases.description: Convert visual identity decisions into practical specifications that developers, writers, and vendors can follow.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Brand Guide
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner prompt for a new guide
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a brand guideline outline for [brand]. Include sections for logo, colors, typography, imagery, voice, messaging, accessibility, and applications.

prompt_templates.title: Define Visual Standards
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate prompt for visual identity documentation
prompt_templates.prompt: Using these brand inputs: [logo rules], [colors], and [fonts], draft clear visual identity standards with usage rules and common mistakes.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Brand Compliance
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt for reviewing existing materials
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this material against our brand guidelines. List compliance issues by severity, explain each issue, and recommend specific fixes.

prompt_templates.title: Build Multi-Channel Governance
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt for mature brand operations
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a governance plan for applying our brand across web, print, social, sales, and internal communications. Include approvals, templates, and review cadence.

output_examples.input: Create a logo usage section for a B2B software brand.
output_examples.output: A section with approved logo versions, clear-space rules, minimum sizes, background rules, and prohibited changes.

output_examples.input: Audit this landing page copy for voice consistency.
output: Rewritten copy that is clearer and more consistent

output_examples.input: Turn these colors and fonts into brand specifications.
output_examples.output: A practical specification covering primary colors, supporting colors, accessibility notes, type hierarchy, and usage examples.

best_practices: Provide real brand assets, exact color values, and approved typefaces before asking for final guidance.
Ask for channel-specific rules so web, print, social, and internal materials use the right constraints.
Review generated guidelines with legal, accessibility, and brand owners before distribution.
anti_patterns: Do not use placeholder company details in published brand documents.
Do not treat design examples as proof of trademark or font licensing rights.
Do not create rules so rigid that teams cannot adapt content for different channels.

faq.question: Can this skill create a full brand guideline document?
faq.answer: Yes. It can structure and draft a complete guide when you provide brand inputs and audience context.

faq.question: Can it audit existing content?
faq.answer: Yes. It can compare materials against provided brand rules and identify consistency issues.

faq.question: Does it design logos?
faq.answer: No. It documents logo usage rules and can guide evaluation, but it does not replace a logo design process.

faq.question: Can it help with accessibility?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for color contrast, typography readability, and accessible application standards.

faq.question: Is it useful for multiple brands?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes workflows for sub-brands, endorsed brands, shared assets, and governance rules.

faq.question: What inputs improve the results?
faq.answer: Provide logo files, color values, font names, audience details, existing examples, and known brand restrictions.
